title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a volunteer weekend at an animal park.
audio_script: Woman: Good morning, and thank you for coming to this meeting about the volunteer weekend at Westfield Animal Park. We’re very pleased so many people want to help. On Saturday morning, the first group will work with the [14]rabbits[/14]. They need quiet volunteers because these animals get frightened easily. Please don’t worry if you haven’t worked with animals before; the keepers will show you what to do. Everyone should wear strong [15]boots[/15], even if the weather is dry, because some paths are muddy all year. We’ll meet at the park [16]café[/16] at ten o’clock. It’s closed to visitors in the morning, so there will be plenty of space for us. After lunch, you’ll be divided into groups of [17]two[/17], as it’s safer to work with another person. You’ll also be given a jacket. Most jackets are green, but volunteers this weekend will wear [18]brown[/18] ones, so visitors can see who is new. At the end of the day, a free [19]bus[/19] will take everyone back to the town centre. It leaves at five thirty, so don’t be late. I hope you all have a useful and enjoyable weekend. The work is not difficult, but you will be outside for part of the day, so check the weather before you come. Please bring your own lunch, although hot drinks will be provided in the staff room. If you enjoy the weekend, you can apply to volunteer during the summer holidays. We especially need people who can come on weekday mornings, when most students are at school or college.  New volunteers should also bring a small bottle of water, because the nearest shop is a long walk from the park entrance.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the first group will work with the rabbits.
The speaker tells volunteers to wear strong boots.
The speaker says they will meet at the park café.
The speaker says volunteers will be divided into groups of two.
The speaker says new volunteers will wear brown jackets.
The speaker says a free bus will take everyone back to the town centre.
